problem: A box contains 5 white balls and 6 black balls.  Five balls are drawn out of the box at random.  What is the probability that they all are white?

classified_questions.question: What is the total number of balls in the box and what is the ratio of white balls to black balls?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How many possible ways are there to draw 5 balls out of the box and why do we use combination to calculate this?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the probability of drawing one white ball out of the box and how does this relate to the probability of drawing 5 white balls?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Can you explain the difference between drawing with replacement and drawing without replacement, and which method is being used in this problem?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: What is the difference in probability of drawing 5 white balls if the box contained 10 white balls and 6 black balls instead?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Is there any difference in probability of drawing 5 white balls if we draw them one by one instead of all at once, and why or why not?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you think of any real-life situations where probability calculation is important, and how can we use the principles we learned in this problem to solve those situations?
classified_questions.label: e
